2604.26097.title: Momentum-Conserving Graph Neural Networks for Deformable Objects
2604.26097.paper_summary: Graph neural networks (GNNs) have emerged as a versatile and efficient option for modeling the dynamic behavior of deformable materials. While GNNs generalize readily to arbitrary shapes, mesh topologies, and material parameters, existing architectures struggle to correctly predict the temporal evolution of key physical quantities such as linear and angular momentum. In this work, we propose MomentumGNN -- a novel architecture designed to accurately track momentum by construction. Unlike existing GNNs that output unconstrained nodal accelerations, our model predicts per-edge stretching and bending impulses which guarantee the preservation of linear and angular momentum. We train our network in an unsupervised fashion using a physics-based loss, and we show that our method outperforms baselines in a number of common scenarios where momentum plays a pivotal role.
2604.26097.paper_summary_zh: 圖形神經網絡（GNNs）已成為建模可變形材料動態行為的多功能且高效的選擇。雖然 GNNs 可以輕鬆地推廣到任意形狀、網格拓撲和材料參數，但現有架構在正確預測關鍵物理量的時間演變方面，如線性和角動量，仍然面臨挑戰。在這項工作中，我們提出了 MomentumGNN——一種新穎的架構，旨在通過構造準確跟踪動量。與現有的 GNNs 輸出不受限制的節點加速度不同，我們的模型預測每條邊的拉伸和彎曲衝量，這保證了線性和角動量的保持。我們以無監督的方式使用基於物理的損失訓練我們的網絡，並顯示我們的方法在動量起關鍵作用的多種常見情境中優於基準。
2604.26097.author: Jiahong Wang et.al.
2604.26097.authors: Jiahong Wang, Logan Numerow, Stelian Coros, Christian Theobalt, Vahid Babaei, Bernhard Thomaszewski
